HNC Tumour Marker Identification — Analysis Report

TCGA-HNSC & GSE65858 | HPV-Negative | All HNC Sites | Combined Score Ranking

Khushi Bhardwaj

2026-04-15

1. Overview

This report summarises the bioinformatic identification of candidate tumour markers for head and neck cancer (HNC), intended to guide wet-lab validation via immunofluorescence (IF) and H&E staining on patient-derived tumouroids.

Analysis parameters:

  • Datasets: TCGA-HNSC (Pan-Can Atlas 2018) and GSE65858
  • HPV status: HPV-negative only
  • Anatomical sites: All HNC sites pooled; lip and nasopharynx excluded from GSE65858
  • Ranking method: Combined score (scaled log2 fold change + scaled log10 base mean) — prioritises genes that are both strongly upregulated AND highly expressed
  • Approach: Independent analysis per dataset, followed by cross-dataset consensus ranking

Sample sizes:

Dataset Tumour Normal Notes
TCGA-HNSC 520 44 Matched adjacent normal tissue; RNA-seq counts
GSE65858 196 0 HPV-negative primary/relapse/secondary tumours; microarray

Note on site annotation: GSE65858 site metadata was insufficient for fine-grained site filtering without prohibitively reducing sample sizes, so all HNC sites were included in both datasets.

2. Analytical Approach

2.1 Why Combined Score Ranking?

Initial analysis ranked genes by log2 fold change alone. While this identifies genes most switched on in tumour vs. normal, it disproportionately favours cancer-testis antigens (e.g. MAGE family) which have near-zero normal tissue expression, inflating their fold change.

A combined score was introduced to balance two complementary metrics:

  • log2 Fold Change — how specifically upregulated is the gene in tumour vs. normal?
  • baseMean (log10-scaled) — how abundantly and consistently is the gene expressed across all samples?
Combined Score = scale(log2FC) + scale(log10(baseMean))

This ensures that clinically and biologically relevant markers with high absolute expression (e.g. MMP1, MMP13, CA9) score alongside highly specific but lower-expressed genes.

3. Dataset 1: TCGA-HNSC

3.1 Differential Expression (DESeq2)

TCGA-HNSC RNA-seq count data was downloaded via TCGAbiolinks and processed using DESeq2. Low-expression genes were removed (< 10 counts in < 10 samples), leaving 29,771 genes for testing.

Result: 5,556 genes were significantly upregulated in tumour vs. normal (padj < 0.05, log2FC > 1).

3.2 Volcano Plot — Combined Score Top 30

library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)

ggplot(tcga_res_df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = neg_log10_p, colour = sig)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.2) +
  geom_label_repel(
    data         = top30_tcga_combined,
    aes(label    = gene_symbol),
    size         = 2.8,
    max.overlaps = 25
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Upregulated"   = "#C0392B",
    "Downregulated" = "#2980B9",
    "NS"            = "grey70"
  )) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  labs(
    title  = "TCGA-HNSC: Tumour vs. Normal (HPV-negative) — Combined Score Top 30",
    x      = "log2 Fold Change",
    y      = "-log10(adjusted p-value)",
    colour = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

Volcano plot of TCGA-HNSC differential expression. Red = significantly upregulated in tumour. Top 30 genes by combined score labelled — note presence of MMP family, CA9, IGF2BP1, CALB1 alongside cancer-testis antigens.

3.3 Top 30 Combined Score Genes

library(knitr)

tcga_combined %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Gene              = gene_symbol,
    `log2FC`          = log2FoldChange,
    `Base Mean`       = baseMean,
    `Combined Score`  = combined_score,
    `Adj. p-value`    = padj
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `log2FC`         = round(`log2FC`, 3),
    `Base Mean`      = round(`Base Mean`, 1),
    `Combined Score` = round(`Combined Score`, 3),
    `Adj. p-value`   = formatC(`Adj. p-value`, format = "e", digits = 2)
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = "Top 30 upregulated genes in TCGA-HNSC tumour vs. normal (HPV-negative), ranked by combined score")

3.4 TCGA Heatmap — Top 30 Combined Score Genes

library(pheatmap)
library(DESeq2)

tcga_vst          <- vst(dds, blind = FALSE)
top30_genes_combined <- top30_tcga_combined$gene[!is.na(top30_tcga_combined$gene)]
heatmap_mat_tcga  <- assay(tcga_vst)[top30_genes_combined, ]
rownames(heatmap_mat_tcga) <- top30_tcga_combined$gene_symbol[
  match(rownames(heatmap_mat_tcga), top30_tcga_combined$gene)
]

anno_col_tcga <- data.frame(
  Condition = tcga_meta$condition,
  row.names = colnames(heatmap_mat_tcga)
)

pheatmap(
  heatmap_mat_tcga,
  annotation_col    = anno_col_tcga,
  annotation_colors = list(Condition = c(Tumour = "#C0392B", Normal = "#2980B9")),
  show_colnames     = FALSE,
  scale             = "row",
  color             = colorRampPalette(c("#2980B9", "white", "#C0392B"))(100),
  main              = "TCGA-HNSC: Top 30 Markers (Combined Score)",
  fontsize_row      = 9,
  clustering_method = "ward.D2"
)

Heatmap of top 30 combined-score genes across TCGA-HNSC tumour and normal samples. Rows z-score scaled. Clear separation between tumour (red) and normal (blue) conditions. MMP/structural genes cluster separately from MAGE cancer-testis antigens.

4. Dataset 2: GSE65858 — Cross-Validation

4.1 Dataset Description

GSE65858 is a microarray dataset (Illumina HumanHT-12 V4.0) comprising 270 HNSCC tumour samples with rich clinical annotation. As this dataset contains tumour samples only, it was used for cross-validation of TCGA-derived markers — confirming which markers are also consistently and highly expressed in an independent HPV-negative cohort.

Cohort composition after filtering:

Tumour Type n
Primary 179
Secondary 14
Relapse 3
Total 196
Anatomical Site n
Cavum Oris 70
Oropharynx 47
Larynx 45
Hypopharynx 30
NA 4

4.2 Cross-Validation Results

Of the TCGA top 30 combined-score genes, 22 were present on the GSE65858 Illumina microarray platform. The remaining 8 were long non-coding RNAs not represented on this array.

The global expression median across GSE65858 was 6.76. Genes with mean expression above this threshold were considered consistently expressed across the independent cohort.

Genes above GSE65858 global median:

Gene GSE65858 Mean Interpretation
MMP1 9.77 Far above median — most consistently expressed gene in cohort
MMP11 8.39 Well above median
MAGEA4 7.54 Above median — best MAGE family candidate
CA9 7.43 Above median — broad tumour expression
MMP13 7.28 Above median
PRAME 7.22 Above median
MAGEA10 6.99 Marginally above median
MAGEA1 6.99 Marginally above median
COL10A1 6.85 Marginally above median

4.3 GSE65858 Consensus Heatmap

heatmap_genes_v2 <- unique(consensus_table_v2$Gene[1:20])
heatmap_mat_gse  <- as.matrix(gse_expr_final[heatmap_genes_v2, ])

anno_col_gse <- data.frame(
  Site = gse_meta_filt$`tumor_site:ch1`,
  Type = gse_meta_filt$`tumor_type:ch1`,
  row.names = colnames(heatmap_mat_gse)
)

pheatmap(
  heatmap_mat_gse,
  annotation_col           = anno_col_gse,
  show_colnames            = FALSE,
  scale                    = "row",
  color                    = colorRampPalette(c("#2980B9", "white", "#C0392B"))(100),
  main                     = "Consensus HNC Markers: GSE65858 (HPV-negative, Combined Score)",
  fontsize_row             = 9,
  clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
  clustering_method        = "ward.D2"
)

Heatmap of top 20 consensus markers across 196 HPV-negative HNC tumour samples in GSE65858. Two biological clusters visible: MMP/structural genes (broadly expressed across all tumour types and sites) vs MAGE cancer-testis antigens (subtype-restricted, patchy expression).

5. Consensus Marker Table

consensus_table_v2 %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Gene                  = Gene,
    `TCGA log2FC`         = TCGA_log2FC,
    `TCGA baseMean`       = TCGA_baseMean,
    `TCGA Combined Score` = TCGA_CombinedScore,
    `GSE65858 Mean`       = GSE65858_meanExpr,
    `Above GSE Median`    = AboveGSEMedian,
    `Final Score`         = FinalScore
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `TCGA log2FC`         = round(`TCGA log2FC`, 3),
    `TCGA baseMean`       = round(`TCGA baseMean`, 1),
    `TCGA Combined Score` = round(`TCGA Combined Score`, 3),
    `GSE65858 Mean`       = round(`GSE65858 Mean`, 3),
    `Final Score`         = round(`Final Score`, 3)
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = "Consensus marker table ranked by final score (TCGA combined score + GSE65858 expression). Above GSE Median = TRUE indicates consistent expression across independent HPV-negative cohort.")

6. Recommended Marker Panel

Based on cross-dataset performance, statistical robustness, expression breadth, and suitability for immunofluorescence detection, the following 5-marker panel is recommended for wet-lab validation:

6.1 Priority Tier 1 — Broad expression, strongest cross-dataset evidence

Rank Gene TCGA baseMean GSE65858 Mean Rationale
1 MMP1 28,786 9.77 Highest expressed gene across both datasets. Matrix metalloproteinase, secreted/extracellular — excellent IF candidate. Well-validated antibodies.
2 MMP13 9,497 7.28 Extremely significant (padj 10⁻⁵¹), very high baseMean, above GSE median. Established role in HNC invasion.
3 MMP11 9,314 8.39 High expression in both datasets. Stromal MMP with strong tumour association.
4 CA9 1,800 7.43 Classic HNSCC hypoxia marker. Cell surface localisation — ideal for IF without permeabilisation. Among the most statistically significant results (padj 10⁻⁸²).

6.2 Priority Tier 2 — Subtype-restricted, cancer-testis antigen

Rank Gene TCGA baseMean GSE65858 Mean Rationale
5 MAGEA4 2,293 7.54 Highest-scoring cancer-testis antigen across both datasets. Above GSE65858 median. Best representative of the MAGE family if tumour subtype markers are of interest. Note: will stain a subset of tumouroids rather than all.

6.3 Exclusion Notes

The following gene categories are not recommended for wet lab validation despite appearing in the computational results:

  • Long non-coding RNAs (LINC01234, LINC02582, AFAP1-AS1, G2E3-AS1) — not detectable by standard IF antibodies
  • MAGE family duplicates (MAGEA1, MAGEA3, MAGEA9, MAGEA10, MAGEA11, MAGEA12, MAGEB2, MAGEC1, MAGEC2) — highly correlated with MAGEA4, subtype-restricted expression, antibody cross-reactivity concerns across family members
  • Nuclear transcription factors (HOXB9, NR5A1, TLX3, NOBOX, SOHLH1) — require nuclear permeabilisation protocols; worth flagging before antibody ordering if Gitali wishes to pursue these

7. Next Steps

  1. Human Protein Atlas & UniProt cross-reference — confirm antibody availability, validation status, and subcellular localisation for MMP1, MMP13, MMP11, CA9, MAGEA4 before ordering
  2. Tumouroid retention check — validate marker expression in GSE223554 and OrganoidDB once data access is confirmed; check whether tumouroids retain 70–90% of tumour expression levels
  3. scRNA-seq validation — use GSE164690 and GSE188737 to verify that top markers are expressed in tumour epithelial cell clusters rather than immune or stromal populations

Analysis performed by Khushi Bhardwaj. Pipeline built in R 4.4.1 using TCGAbiolinks, DESeq2, GEOquery, limma, org.Hs.eg.db, pheatmap, ggplot2, and associated Bioconductor/CRAN packages. All datasets publicly available; TCGA-HNSC accessed via GDC portal, GSE65858 via NCBI GEO.
